reasoning: This kernel does very little math per iteration and performs one load from `a`, one load from `b`, and one scattered store to `out[bucket]`; arithmetic intensity is far below the A10G ridge point of 62 FLOP/byte, so it is not compute-bound. The dominant issue is the random, non-coalesced write to `out`, which causes poor cache locality and long memory-scoreboard waits; with many resident warps (`4096x256` launch), occupancy is not the main problem, and DRAM bandwidth is unlikely to be driven to >50% peak because accesses are latency-dominated rather than streaming-bandwidth-limited.
raw_response:

The dominant issue is the random, non-coalesced write to `out`, which causes poor cache locality and long memory-scoreboard waits; with many resident warps (`4096x256` launch), occupancy is not the main problem, and DRAM bandwidth is unlikely to be driven to >50% peak because accesses are latency-dominated rather than streaming-bandwidth-limited.
